notes: Evals run 2026-04-28 (simulation). 3 scenarios: multi-persona (4/4=1.0), suggestions (4/4=1.0), model-selection (2/3=0.67). Gap in model-selection: synthesis report template lacks a 'Model:' field — skill captures the choice but does not surface it in feature-counsel-report.md output.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 2 explicitly requires launching 8 Task agents in parallel (all in a single message), one per each named persona.

assertions.id: each analyzes specs from their perspective
assertions.note: Sub-agent prompt template includes '{PERSPECTIVE}' filled in per the agent assignment table with a distinct Analysis Focus per persona.
Sub-agent output format mandates a 'Missing Features' section — a table listing feature, priority MUST/SHOULD/COULD, persona rationale, and spec section.
SKILL.md Step 3 instructs writing synthesis to '{PROJECT}/openspec/feature-counsel-report.md' with the full markdown template.

runs.notes: Parallel 8-agent launch, per-persona perspective focus, required output format, and synthesis step are all explicitly specified in SKILL.md.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: suggests features with clear user value
assertions.note: Sub-agent output format requires 'Missing Features' table with Feature, Priority, 'Why I need this' (in persona voice), and Spec section columns.

assertions.id: links suggestions to personas who need them
assertions.note: Synthesis template's 'Consensus Features' table includes a 'Suggested by' column listing persona names throughout the report.
Each agent output format includes 'Compliance/Standards Gaps' section. Agent 4 (Noor) focuses on BIO2/ENSIA/AVG; Agent 5 (Annemarie) focuses on GEMMA/Common Ground/NLGov.

assertions.id: suggests OpenSpec changes for top features
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 3 synthesis template includes 'Potential OpenSpec Changes' section with Change Name, Description, Related Personas, and Estimated Complexity columns.

runs.notes: Structured output templates ensure every suggestion is specific, persona-attributed, compliance-aware, and actionable as an OpenSpec change.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 1.5 defines an AskUserQuestion with Haiku/Sonnet/Opus options, tradeoffs table, and stores the choice as {MODEL}.
Step 2 instructs 'model: "{MODEL}" (from Step 1.5)' in the parallel launch, so Haiku choice → all 8 agents run with model: "haiku".

assertions.id: reports which model each persona used
assertions.note: Neither the sub-agent output template nor synthesis report template includes a 'Model:' field. Report header shows Date, Method, Personas — but not the model used. Gap in skill's output spec.

runs.notes: Skill correctly accepts and propagates the model choice. Gap: feature-counsel-report.md has no 'Model:' field in its header template — add one line to the report template in SKILL.md to close this.
